# 005 Static Semantics

Status: draft
Source of truth: libs/api/workflow-language/src/core/static-semantics/diagnostic.ts

## Purpose

Document reusable validation rules over WorkflowIR.

## Normative Model

- Static diagnostics use stable IDs.

- Definitions parse-time validation uses shared WorkflowIR static checks.

- Run creation uses the same static checks before persisting jobs and steps.

<!-- generated by libs/api/workflow-language/scripts/generate-formalization-docs.ts; do not edit generated sections directly -->
<!-- generated:start -->
- Diagnostic registry owner: `libs/api/workflow-language/src/core/static-semantics/diagnostic.ts`.

- Static check owner: `libs/api/workflow-language/src/core/static-semantics/validate-workflow-ir.ts`.

- `SS001_UNKNOWN_JOB_DEPENDENCY`: dependency edge references a missing job.

- `SS002_SELF_JOB_DEPENDENCY`: dependency edge points from a job to itself.

- `SS003_CYCLIC_JOB_DEPENDENCY`: dependency graph contains a cycle.

- Reference diagnostics are reported before cycle diagnostics because cycle analysis requires resolvable edge endpoints.

- Static diagnostics use `JobIR.sourceName` when reporting author-facing job names and paths.
<!-- generated:end -->

## Examples

A surface `needs: ghost` entry normalizes to an unresolved IR edge and returns `SS001_UNKNOWN_JOB_DEPENDENCY`.

A cycle such as `a -> b -> c -> a` returns `SS003_CYCLIC_JOB_DEPENDENCY`.

## Adding Or Changing This Concept

Add diagnostic IDs, tests, docs, and call sites for each new semantic rule.

## Deferred Work

- Rich runner fact validation.
